rulings.Case Title: MINOR LENTFER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, January 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Metalclad¿s motion for summary adjudication on punitive damages is granted. Metalclad shifted burden of production due to plaintiff¿s factually devoid interrogatory responses. Lentfer¿s evidence fails to create a triable issue of material fact as to whether Metalclad acted with fraud malice or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Metalclad withdrew its motion as to the remainder of the issues =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CLIFFORD HOLBROOK et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Deft Fluor Corporation'S Ntc Of Mo & Mo To Stay Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, January 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Joinder In Defendant Fluor Corporation's motion to stay or dismiss based On Forum Non Conveniens is unopposed to the extent it asks for a stay conditioned upon a finding of jurisdiction by the alternative forum. A stay is granted pending determination of jurisdiction over all defendants by the Utah court. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BEVERLEY JAMES LOWE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, January 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC.'S Motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication (issues 1-5) as to exposure, component parts doctrine and sophisticated intermediary are denied. Plaintiff¿s declaration and deposition testimony of Beverley Lowe and Buffalo Pumps¿ interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Lowe was exposed to original packing and gaskets of buffalo pumps aboard the USS Thomas Edison. The Fening declaration of Felling creates a triable issue whether the original packing and gaskets contained asbestos. Buffalo Pumps has not established all the elements of a sophisticated intermediary defense. There is no evidence that Buffalo Pumps knew of the Navy¿s level of sophistication or that it would have been reasonable for Buffalo Pumps to rely on the Navy to protect its employees. (Willis v. Raymark Industries, Inc. (4th Cir. 1990) 905 F.2d 793.) Buffalo Pumps¿ motion for summary adjudication(issues 6-7) as to false representation and punitive damages is granted based on plaintiff¿s concession on these issues. =(220/HEK)
